title: Spatial Discretization Schemes - Face Value Interpolation
subtitle: From PDE to Algebraic Equations in OpenFOAM

base.description: Abstract base class for all surface interpolation schemes. Holds reference to fvMesh.

direct_schemes.description: Centred interpolation using geometric distance-based weights

limited_schemes.description: Intermediate base for flux-based schemes requiring faceFlux
First-order upwind - selects value based on flow direction
NVD/TVD framework - blends high-order and upwind using limiter

limiters.description: vanLeer TVD limiter - smooth limiter function

vanLeer_limiter.critical_note: Uses mag(r) function, NOT plain r

upwind_weights.description: Returns 1.0 for positive (outward) flux, 0.0 for negative (inward)

linear_weights.description: Returns geometric weights based on distance from face to cell centers

central_differencing.note: w is geometric weight (distance from face to owner / total distance)

abstract_weights.description: Pure virtual - must be implemented by derived schemes

virtual_interpolate.description: Returns face-interpolated field with optional correction

upwind_limiter.description: Upwind has no limiter - always uses first-order

vanLeer_limiter_method.signature: scalar limiter(const scalar, const scalar faceFlux, ...)
vanLeer_limiter_method.description: Computes limiter by first calculating r, then applying formula

key_concepts.face_interpolation: Converting cell-centered values to face-centered values for flux calculation
key_concepts.weighting_factor: Coefficient w determining blend between owner and neighbor values
key_concepts.peclet_number: Pe = \frac{\rho u L}{D} - ratio of convective to diffusive transport
key_concepts.tvd_principle: Total Variation Diminishing - prevents non-physical oscillations
key_concepts.numerical_diffusion: False diffusion introduced by first-order schemes smears gradients
key_concepts.boundedness: Property that solution stays within physical bounds (0 to 1 for VOF)

part1.title: Core Theory - From Continuous to Discrete
topics: Why face value interpolation is needed in FVM
Central differencing derivation from Taylor series

part2.title: Physical Challenge - Convection-Dominated Problems
topics: Why central differencing fails for high Pe

part4.title: Quality Assurance - Scheme Selection
topics: Scheme selection guidelines for different physics

part5.title: R410A Application
topics: Why scheme matters for evaporator simulation

learning_objectives: Derive central differencing formula from geometric interpolation
Explain why upwind is stable but introduces numerical diffusion
Calculate Peclet number and predict scheme behavior
Trace through OpenFOAM's interpolation scheme class hierarchy
Implement custom TVD limiter using LimitedScheme framework

connections.from_day_02: Builds on Gauss divergence theorem and face flux calculation
connections.to_day_10: Essential for VOF alpha transport equation discretization
connections.to_day_52: Interface sharpening techniques rely on TVD schemes

concept_check: Derive central differencing weights from geometric interpolation
Explain relationship between Peclet number and scheme stability
Compare TVD limiters: vanLeer vs Minmod characteristics
code_verification: Locate and read surfaceInterpolationScheme base class
Verify vanLeer limiter uses mag(r) not r at line 80
Trace weight calculation through linear scheme
